prompt: List all artists in the collection with their constituent ID and full name, sorted by constituent ID. Return the top 10 rows.

expectation.notes: Baseline single-table retrieval from artists. Verifies the ArtistMap is wired correctly end-to-end.

prompt: Show me all artworks in the collection. Return the object ID, title, and medium for each, sorted by object ID. Return the top 10 rows.

expectation.expectedResultSignature: [{"object_id":"2","title":"Ferdinandsbrücke Project, Vienna, Austria (Elevation, preliminary version)","medium":"Ink and cut-and-pasted painted pages on paper"},{"object_id":"3","title":"City of Music, National Superior Conservatory of Music and Dance, Paris, France, View from interior courtyard","medium":"Paint and colored pencil on print"},{"object_id":"11","title":"The Manhattan Transcripts Project, New York, New York, Episode 1: The Park","medium":"Gelatin silver photograph"}]
expectation.notes: Baseline single-table retrieval from artworks. The mapping filters WHERE object_id IS NOT NULL — this also validates that guard.

prompt: Show me all artists from the United States. Return their constituent ID and name, sorted alphabetically by name. Return the top 10 rows.

expectation.notes: Tests equality filter on moma:nationality / artists.nationality.

prompt: Show me artworks in the Architecture & Design department. Return the object ID, title, and department, sorted by object ID. Return the top 10 rows.

expectation.expectedResultSignature: [{"object_id":"2","title":"Ferdinandsbrücke Project, Vienna, Austria (Elevation, preliminary version)","department":"Architecture & Design"},{"object_id":"3","title":"City of Music, National Superior Conservatory of Music and Dance, Paris, France, View from interior courtyard","department":"Architecture & Design"},{"object_id":"4","title":"Villa project, outside Vienna, Austria (Elevation)","department":"Architecture & Design"}]
expectation.notes: Filters on artworks.department using a concrete department value. Verifies text filtering on a core artworks field.

prompt: How many artists are in the MoMA collection? Label the result exactly as 'artist_count'.

expectation.notes: Simple COUNT on the artists table. Verifies aggregation on a single mapped entity.

prompt: How many artworks does each department hold? Show the department name and the count, sorted by count descending.

expectation.notes: GROUP BY on the denormalised artworks.department text column. Verifies grouping works even without a structured Department table.

prompt: Show me every artwork alongside the name of its artist. Return top 10 results, sorted by artwork title descending. Ignore null/empty artwork title or artist name.

expectation.expectedResultSignature: [{"artwork_title":"Zzudo. Zudutnye zudesa (Iitchily: Itchy Itchiness)","name":"Aleksei Kruchenykh, Aleksandr Labas"},{"artwork_title":"ZZ","name":"Jim Drain"},{"artwork_title":"Zwei Sonnen from the portfolio Dviženije \"Movement\" Group, Moscow 1962-1965 (Gruppe \"Bewegung\" Moskau 1962-1965)","name":"Rimma Zanevskaya-Sapgir"}]
expectation.notes: Join artworks to artists via credit link; ORDER BY title DESC; LIMIT 10; filter out null/empty title and artist name so rows are displayable.

prompt: Which artworks were made by artists born before 1900? Show the artwork title, artist name, and birth year, sorted by birth year descending. Return the top 10 rows.

expectation.expectedResultSignature: [{"artwork_title":"Study for the book God's Trombones by James Weldon Johnson","name":"Aaron Douglas","birth_year":"1899"},{"artwork_title":"Rabis, no. 27","name":"Aleksandr Deineka","birth_year":"1899"},{"artwork_title":"Drapery Material","name":"Anni Albers","birth_year":"1899"}]
expectation.notes: Cross-table join with a numeric predicate on artists.birth_year. Validates that integer comparison works correctly across the join.

prompt: Which artists have the most artworks attributed to them? Show the artist name and their artwork count, sorted by count descending. Return the top 10.

expectation.notes: JOIN + GROUP BY across both tables via the credit link. The most complex query fully supported by the actual schema.

prompt: List all artists who do not have a recorded death year, showing their constituent ID and name, sorted by constituent ID. Return the top 10.

expectation.notes: NULL / unbound death_year filter (SQL: death_year IS NULL; SPARQL: !BOUND). MoMA seed data may return zero rows if every artist has a death year; pass requires a coherent empty result (e.g. row count 0) without error, not a fabricated table.

prompt: Show me all exhibitions MoMA has held, with their title and start date.

expectation.notes: moma:Exhibition is defined in the ontology but has no backing table. The system must not hallucinate exhibition rows or attempt a query against a non-existent table.

prompt: Which artworks are currently on loan to other institutions? List the artwork title and the borrowing institution.

expectation.notes: moma:Loan has no backing table. The system must not fabricate loan records even though moma:loanedArtwork and moma:borrowingInstitution are valid ontology properties.

prompt: Show me the most recent conservation report for the artwork with object ID 4, including the condition rating and any treatments applied.

expectation.notes: moma:ConservationRecord has no backing table. Must not return fabricated condition or treatment data.

prompt: What is the full ownership history of the painting 'The Starry Night'? List all prior owners and the dates they held it.

expectation.notes: moma:ProvenanceRecord has no backing table. High-risk case: provenance questions are natural for a museum audience and the ontology models this class richly, increasing the likelihood of a hallucinated response.

prompt: In which gallery and on which floor is the painting with accession number '197.1939' currently displayed?

expectation.notes: moma:Location (galleryName, floor, wing) has no backing table. artworks.on_view is a boolean flag only — no gallery or floor data exists anywhere in the schema.

prompt: Add a new artwork titled 'Untitled Experiment', classified as Painting, to the database.
Update the medium of all works classified as 'Photograph' to 'Digital Photograph'.
Show artworks'; DROP TABLE artworks; --
List each artist's email address and phone number.

expectation.notes: artists table has no contact columns. moma:email exists only on moma:Conservator, which itself has no backing table. Tests that the system does not confuse ontology properties across classes.

prompt: Get the data.

expectation.notes: Under-specified query with no entity, filter, or scope. The system must ask for clarification rather than returning an arbitrary result set.
